expectations.text: Identifies the issue as ambiguous spacing or unclear proximity between related elements
expectations.evidence: Section header: 'Ambiguous Spacing — Significant. When the space between a label and its input is the same as the space between two unrelated form groups, the eye has no visual cue to determine which label belongs to which field'

expectations.text: Explains that related elements (label + input) should be closer to each other than to unrelated elements
expectations.evidence: 'This is the Gestalt principle of proximity: elements should be closer to what they belong to than to what they don't'

expectations.text: Gives specific spacing values for label-to-input (small) vs group-to-group (larger)
expectations.evidence: Table: 'Label → its input: 4px–8px; Between form groups: 16px–24px; Between page sections: 48px–96px' with CSS example: `.form-label { margin-bottom: 4px; }`, `.form-group { margin-bottom: 24px; }`

suggestions.reason: Consider adding an assertion about spacing scale (base-4 or base-8) — the skill teaches this as a system, not just the label/input proximity fix

eval_feedback.overall: Solid. All three assertions pass cleanly. The skill's use of the Refactoring UI term 'Ambiguous Spacing' as a named principle is a nice discriminator.
